21.correct: Water has its maximum density at 4 degrees Celsius; below this it expands again, which is why ice floats.

wrong.A: At 0 C water is freezing into ice, which is less dense.
wrong.C: At 25 C water is less dense than at 4 C.
wrong.D: At 100 C water is at its boiling point and far less dense.

21.fact: This 'anomalous expansion' of water lets aquatic life survive under ice in winter.

22.correct: Nidhi Tewari became the private secretary to Prime Minister Modi in March 2025.

wrong.B: Sunita Williams is a NASA astronaut.
wrong.C: Danish Mahajan is not the appointee.
wrong.D: Anju Rathi Rana was appointed Law Secretary, a different post.

22.fact: The PM's office (PMO) has several private secretaries and officers on special duty.

23.correct: Anju Rathi Rana was appointed the Union Law Secretary in March 2025.

wrong.B: Shubhanshu Shukla is an astronaut (Gaganyaan/Axiom), not the Law Secretary.
wrong.C: K. Vinod Chandran is a judge, not the Law Secretary.
wrong.D: Danish Mahajan is not the appointee.

23.fact: The Law Secretary heads the Department of Legal Affairs in the Ministry of Law and Justice.

24.correct: Rajaraja I became the king of the Chola Empire in 985 CE.

wrong.B: 1008 falls within his reign, not its start.

24.fact: Rajaraja I built the great Brihadisvara Temple at Thanjavur.

25.correct: In August 2025 the UPSC launched 'Pratibha Setu' to connect top (non-recommended but meritorious) candidates with employers, including the private sector.

wrong.B: CBSE did not launch Pratibha Setu.

25.fact: Pratibha Setu (earlier PESB/PDDS) helps candidates who clear UPSC stages but miss final selection find jobs.

26.correct: SWAYATT stands for 'Startups, Women and Youth Advantage Through e-Transactions', an initiative on the Government e-Marketplace (GeM).

wrong.A: 'Strengthening Women and Youth Against Trade Threats' is incorrect.
wrong.C: 'Supporting Workers and Young Adults Through Training' is incorrect.
wrong.D: 'Sustainable Work and Youth Alliance for Technological Trade' is incorrect.

26.fact: SWAYATT promotes inclusion of startups, women and youth in public procurement on GeM.

27.correct: In Tamil Nadu, 'Mayil Aattam' (peacock dance) is performed by dancers wearing peacock costumes in temples of Lord Murugan.

wrong.A: Gambhira is a folk form of West Bengal.
wrong.B: Pung Cholom is a Manipuri drum dance.
wrong.C: Theru Koothu is Tamil street theatre, not the peacock dance.

27.fact: The peacock is the vahana (mount) of Lord Murugan.

28.correct: The 'Hareli' festival is celebrated in Chhattisgarh, marking the agricultural/monsoon season.

wrong.A: It is not an Assam festival.
wrong.B: It is not from Himachal Pradesh.
wrong.D: It is not a Gujarat festival.

28.fact: Hareli ('greenery') is one of the earliest festivals of the Chhattisgarhi calendar, when farmers worship their tools and cattle.

29.correct: The Haryana government planned to develop Asia's largest jungle safari in the Aravalli hills.

wrong.B: Rajasthan is not developing this particular project.
wrong.D: Delhi is not developing the Aravalli jungle safari.

29.fact: The Aravalli range acts as a green barrier against the spread of the Thar Desert.

30.correct: The Kosi-Mechi intra-state river-linking project is associated with Bihar.

wrong.B: It is not in Madhya Pradesh.
wrong.D: It is not in Tamil Nadu.

30.fact: The Kosi is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar' for its frequent flooding.

31.correct: ISRO's PSLV-XL configuration has a gross lift-off weight of about 320 tonnes.

wrong.B: 1040 tonnes is far too heavy for the PSLV.

31.fact: The PSLV-XL uses larger strap-on boosters for heavier payloads, such as Chandrayaan-1 and Mangalyaan.

32.correct: AstroSat has contributed to both - detecting ultraviolet emissions from distant galaxies (I) and studying X-ray polarisation in supernova remnants (II) - so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.A: 'Only I' omits the X-ray polarisation work.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong as both have been achieved.

32.fact: AstroSat (2015) is India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory.

33.correct: IIT Madras is leading the development of the indigenous 'Shakti' microprocessor.

wrong.A: IIT Bombay is not leading the Shakti project.
wrong.B: IIT Delhi is not the lead.
wrong.D: IIT Kanpur is not the lead.

33.fact: Shakti is based on the open-source RISC-V instruction set architecture.

34.correct: In December 2024 IIT Ropar unveiled a cost-effective Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) gateway with advanced data security for IoT applications.

wrong.A: IIT Delhi was not credited with this.
wrong.C: IIT Madras was not the developer cited.
wrong.D: IIT Bombay was not the developer.

34.fact: BLE gateways link low-power IoT sensors to the internet/cloud.

35.correct: The BIMSTEC Youth Summit 2025 was held in Gandhinagar (Gujarat) from 7-11 February 2025.

wrong.A: It was not held in Chennai.
wrong.C: It was not held in Dhaka.
wrong.D: It was not held in Colombo.

35.fact: BIMSTEC links countries around the Bay of Bengal, including India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Myanmar and Thailand.

36.correct: Dev Meena set the national pole-vault record of 5.40 metres in 2025.

wrong.A: 2019 is not when this record was set.

36.fact: Pole vaulting is a track-and-field event requiring speed, strength and technique.

37.correct: In athletics, all running events come under track events (I), so 'Only I' is correct; there is no separate category called 'ground events'.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; running events are track events.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since running events are only track events.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since running events are track events.

37.fact: Athletics is broadly divided into track events (running) and field events (jumps and throws).

38.correct: The magnetic field inside a current-carrying solenoid is made of parallel, straight, uniform field lines, while outside it resembles a bar magnet (field lines looping around) - among the options, 'parallel straight lines inside, circles outside' best describes it.

wrong.A: The field is not random and irregular; it is well-ordered.
wrong.C: Pure concentric circles describe the field around a straight wire, not a solenoid.

38.fact: A solenoid carrying current behaves like a bar magnet, the basis of electromagnets.

39.correct: The instrument shown is the 'Jantar', a traditional plucked string instrument (associated with Rajasthan folk traditions).

wrong.B: A trumpet is a brass wind instrument.
wrong.C: The Nadaswaram is a South Indian wind instrument.
wrong.D: The Mridangam is a South Indian percussion drum.

39.fact: The Jantar is a stick-zither with gourd resonators, used by folk balladeers.

40.correct: Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), a constituent of petroleum, is used as a fuel for homes (cooking) and industry.

wrong.A: Ointments, candles and vaseline come from petroleum jelly/wax, not LPG.
wrong.B: Heavy motor vehicles and generators mainly use diesel, not LPG.
wrong.C: Paints and road surfacing relate to bitumen/other fractions, not LPG.

40.fact: LPG is mainly propane and butane, stored under pressure as a liquid.
